keyMetrics.arr: >$1B total ARR (Finance: >$600M; Sales & Marketing: >$200M; Supply Chain: ~$200M)
keyMetrics.operatingMargin: Rule of 55 (growth % + FCF margin; positive EBITDA, up from negative at go-private)
keyMetrics.customers: ~half of Fortune 50; 200+ customers with >$1M ACV (>50% of aggregate ARR); ~300 new logos added past year

aiCommentary: CEO Charles Gottdiener is positioning Anaplan around AI and 'decision excellence.' Key message: 'Anaplan Intelligence is AI built into the heart of the platform, grounded in years of enterprise experience.' Strategic priorities: (1) $500M multi-year product innovation roadmap; (2) 40% increase in software engineering headcount; (3) expansion into Supply Chain as fastest-growing BU; (4) Fluence Technologies acquisition (financial close/consolidation); (5) confidential IPO filing underway as of Jan 2026. Gottdiener thesis (McKinsey interview, June 2024): companies that run tighter decision cycles outperform peers by ~14pp of TSR.
demandCommentary: No direct public macro commentary (private company). Implied demand signal: ~300 new logos and 200+ customers crossing the $1M ACV threshold suggest healthy enterprise demand. Supply Chain described as fastest-growing BU, indicating successful cross-sell beyond the core Finance buyer. Asian market expansion cited as a growth driver. IPO preparation signals management and sponsor confidence in demand trajectory.
guidanceNotes: No formal guidance (private company). Rule of 55 metric suggests growing at a meaningful rate while cash flow positive. $500M R&D commitment is the key forward-looking signal.
